name: Hybrid MPI/OpenMP On Slurm
summary: Plan and verify hybrid MPI/OpenMP task and thread layouts on Slurm.
description: This skill helps users and support teams choose a small Slurm task shape for hybrid MPI/OpenMP applications, align cpus-per-task with OMP_NUM_THREADS, and collect evidence about rank, thread, and CPU binding before scaling simulation or performance workloads.

inputs.name: account
inputs.description: Site-specific Slurm account or allocation for the short hybrid layout job.

inputs.name: partition
inputs.description: Slurm partition or queue for the short hybrid layout job.

inputs.name: task_shape
inputs.description: Nodes, tasks per node, cpus per task, total MPI tasks, and OpenMP threads per rank.

inputs.name: mpi_module
inputs.description: Optional MPI compiler/runtime module to load before compiling the probe.

inputs.name: openmp_policy
inputs.description: OMP_NUM_THREADS, OMP_PLACES, OMP_PROC_BIND, and Slurm --cpu-bind policy for the small probe.

inputs.name: application_command
inputs.description: Real hybrid application command to substitute after the probe layout is reviewed.

outputs.name: hybrid_layout_plan
outputs.description: Dry-run compile and srun command lines plus Slurm and OpenMP environment settings.

outputs.name: topology_report
outputs.description: Node, Slurm, MPI, OpenMP, and CPU topology evidence written before launch.

outputs.name: rank_thread_report
outputs.description: TSV report mapping MPI ranks and OpenMP threads to host, CPU, visible affinity, and OpenMP settings.

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Submit the wrapper with RUN_HYBRID_PROBE=0 to review the plan, then set RUN_HYBRID_PROBE=1 on a short test partition and confirm rank-thread-affinity.tsv has one row per rank/thread pair.
